differential_diagnoses: Primary angiitis of the central nervous system (PACNS)
Neurodegenerative disease (Alzheimer's disease with comorbid CAA)
Metastatic malignancy to the leptomeninges/limbic system
next_steps: Send CSF for autoimmune encephalitis panel (neuronal surface antibodies including LGI1 and CASPR2) and paraneoplastic antibody panel (including anti-Hu)
Perform whole-body PET-CT to search for occult malignancy
Initiate pulse corticosteroid therapy given clinical suspicion for inflammatory/autoimmune etiology
Continue antiseizure therapy (e.g., levetiracetam or lacosamide)
Repeat MRI brain in 4-6 weeks to assess for radiological response to treatment
Consider brain biopsy if clinical/radiological stability is not achieved with empiric treatment
